rulings.Case Title: SCHAPIRO-THORN INC., VS. DANIEL L. MITCHELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Hearing On Third Party Claim And Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 3, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT DANIEL MITCHELL'S Petition For Hearing On Third Party Claim And Order. Continued to July 9, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 3, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL L. PRICE VS. ROBERT E. WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos And Ntc Of Mtn For Order Determining Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 3, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT ROBERT WHITE'S Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Denied. Claimant has not demonstrated that the subject trust is not subject to enforcement of the money judgment pursuant to Fin. Code §17410. Claimant has not demonstrated that the money judgment arises out of any claim against a trustee acting as an escrow agent. (Fin. Code § 17410(a).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LUKE D. BRUGNARA VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 3, 2014, Line 3, RESPONDENT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Art. XIII sec. 32 of the California Constitution provides: "No legal or equitable process shall issue in any proceeding in any court against this State or any officer thereof to prevent or enjoin the collection of any tax. After payment of a tax claimed to be illegal, an action may be maintained to recover the tax paid, with interest, in such manner as may be provided by the Legislature." "A taxpayer may not go into court and obtain adjudication of the validity of a tax which is due but not yet paid. The important public policy behind this constitutional provision 'is to allow revenue collection to continue during litigation so that essential public services dependent on the funds are not unnecessarily interrupted.'" State Bd. of Equalization v. Sup. Crt. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 633, 638. In this case, petitioner's petition and attachments facially demonstrate that he has not paid the tax. Accordingly, the action fails to state a cause of action. See CCP 430.10(e). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SYDNEY SAIDYAN VS. AMERICAN LASER CENTERS OF CALIFORNIA LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication As To The First Amended Complaint Ntc Of Lodging Of Records Vol 1 Of 2 And 2 Of 2
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 3, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANTS AMERICAN LASER SKINCARE LLC, BULLUS ALC OF CALIFORNIA LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/Adjudication As To The First Amended Complaint. The motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to meet its initial burden of production. The motion for summary judgment is denied because plaintiff's claims for damages are not limited to conduct that occurred prior to the asset purchase that occurred on February 3, 2012. Plaintiff also alleges that defendant is liable for its direct misconduct that took place from February 2012 through June of 2012. The court cannot grant summary adjudication that defendant is not liable for any pre-asset purchase conduct of the debtor. CCP 437c(f)(1) provides that summary adjudication can only be granted where it completely disposes of the cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC, et al VS. HAROLD WONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 3, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFFS LEM WONG, L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. The cross-complaint alleges standing/injury. Even if the court grants Father's request for judicial notice, those documents do not demonstrate that Son lacks standing. Paragraph 10 of the amended cross-complaint clearly alleges that Son has an ownership interest in the LLC and Son obtained that interest after December 24, 2005 (the date referenced in Ex. B to the Operating Agreement). Father improperly raises factual issues and is contesting the truth of Son's allegations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC, et al VS. HAROLD WONG et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 3, 2014, Line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT LEM WONG'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is denied. rulings.Case Title: DIANA ANDERSON VS. BRENNER, VICTORIA, AKA VICTORIA MARIE GAIST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion For Order Compelling Victoria Brenner To 1) Appear For Dposition And Produce Documents Thereat; And 2) Sign, Verify And Further Respond To A) Form Interrogatories, Set One; And B) Request For Admissions, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 3, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DIANA ANDERSON's Motion For Order Compelling Victoria Brenner To 1) Appear For Dposition And Produce Documents Thereat; And 2) Sign, Verify And Further Respond To A) Form Interrogatories, Set One; And B) Request For Admissions, Set One Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the motion in its entirety. No costs or sanctions are awarded. Responding party shall have 15 calendar days to supply the information sought. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt
